title: pycreus elliottianus
intro: Pycreus elliottianus, also known as Elliott's sedge or Elliott's grass, is a plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pains, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Pycreus elliottianus can be prepared as a herbal remedy by infusing its leaves in hot water to make a tea, or by using its roots to create a decoction for medicinal purposes.
